items.briefing:
Recent reports highlight growing concerns about sophisticated impersonation scams targeting Australians. CommBank has issued a warning about increasingly convincing scams, while the AFR has reported on AI-powered fraud that successfully deceived one of Australia's largest banks. These stories remind us that scammers are using advanced technology to make their approaches more believable, whether through fake emails, phone calls, or messages that appear to come from trusted organisations. Education sector staff should be particularly vigilant, as training organisations often handle sensitive student and financial information that criminals find valuable.

On a more positive note, Australian organisations are investing in stronger cyber defences. Australia Post has partnered with Alpha Level to enhance its AI-driven threat detection capabilities, while Macquarie Technology Group and Macquarie University have announced a research collaboration focused on cyber security and cloud computing. These partnerships demonstrate the importance of ongoing investment in security measures across both the public and private sectors.

**Today's tip:** Before acting on any urgent request for money or personal information—whether by email, phone, or text—pause and verify the request through official channels. Look up the organisation's contact details independently rather than using any links or numbers provided in the message.
